name: San Marcos and Txoritokieta Loop
name_original: Vuelta de Castel de San Markos
description: This difficult 5.6-mile mountain bike loop in Lau-Haizeta Park offers 950 feet of climbing and takes just over an hour.
region_intro: Hiking around Errenteria offers diverse landscapes, from mountainous terrain to verdant forests and coastal proximity. The region is characterized by the Aiako Harria Natural Park, which includes the "Three Crowns" massif, and the Jaizkibel Mountain, providing panoramic views. The Oiartzun River flows through the area, contributing to scenic routes alongside historical sites. Best hiking trails around Errenteria The most popular hiking route is Urkabe, Arkale, and Beloaga Castle Loop, an 8.0 miles (12.9 km) trail that takes 3 hours 48 minutes to complete. This route features historical castle ruins and varied terrain. Another top favourite among local hikers is San Markos Trail (SL-GI 3), a moderate 4.2 miles (6.8 km) path. It offers views from the Fort of San Marcos over San Sebastián and Pasaia Bay. Local hikers also love the View of Pasaia Bay – Ancient Stone Aqueduct loop from Galtzaraborda, a 10.9 miles (17.5 km) trail leading through coastal landscapes, often completed in about 5 hours 1 minute. Hiking around Errenteria is defined by mountainous terrain, dense woodlands, and coastal paths. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to challenging ascents. The routes in Errenteria are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 6000 hikers have used komoot to explore Errenteria's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Errenteria?
A: There are over 70 hiking trails available around Errenteria, offering a wide range of experiences from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The komoot community has explored these routes over 6,000 times.

Q: What kind of natural landscapes can I expect to see while hiking in Errenteria?
A: Errenteria is characterized by diverse natural landscapes, including the mountainous terrain of the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/295029" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Jaizkibel Summit&lt;/a&gt; and the Aiako Harria Natural Park. You'll find verdant forests, the scenic Oiartzun River Valley, and proximity to the coastline, offering stunning views of the Atlantic and Pasaia Bay.

Q: Are there any easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families in Errenteria?
A: Yes, Errenteria offers several easy hiking options. While specific family-friendly routes are not detailed, the region has 11 easy tours. Many trails provide pleasant, shaded walks, such as those leading to the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1841170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aitzondo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;, which is generally a pleasant and shaded walk.

Q: Can I find circular hiking routes in Errenteria?
A: Yes, many routes in Errenteria are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/3992937" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Urdaburu Summit (600 m) – Collado de Lizarregi loop from Mugaritz&lt;/a&gt; offers a rewarding moderate challenge. Another popular option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/5346229" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Senokozulua Lighthouse – Pasai Donibane loop from Galtzaraborda&lt;/a&gt;, which provides coastal views.

Q: What historical landmarks or points of interest can I discover on hikes around Errenteria?
A: Hiking in Errenteria often combines natural beauty with historical elements. You can visit the Fort of San Marcos, an 1880 military construction offering sweeping views, or explore the ancient &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33905066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ancient Stone Aqueduct&lt;/a&gt;. The area also features megalithic routes with dolmens and the historical Arditurri Mines near Oiartzun.

Q: Are there any hikes that feature waterfalls in the Errenteria area?
A: Yes, you can hike to the impressive &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1841170" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Aitzondo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. The SL-GI-1006 trail leads directly to it, passing by the Irugurutzeta kilns, making for a rewarding and often shaded walk.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Errenteria?
A: The hiking trails around Errenteria are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.6 stars from over 800 reviews. Hikers often praise the diverse terrain, from mountainous ascents to scenic coastal paths, and the combination of natural beauty with historical sites.

Q: Are there any trails with good viewpoints for panoramic scenery?
A: Absolutely. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1323182707/san-markos-trail-sl-gi-3" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Markos Trail (SL-GI 3)&lt;/a&gt; offers excellent views from the Fort of San Marcos over San Sebastián and Pasaia Bay. The Aiako Harria Natural Park's "Three Crowns" massif also provides panoramic 360° views of the Southern Basque Country and the Labour coastline.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Errenteria?
A: Errenteria offers hiking opportunities throughout much of the year due to its varied terrain. While specific seasonal recommendations are not provided, the region's mild climate generally makes spring and autumn ideal for comfortable hiking. Summer can also be pleasant, especially on shaded forest trails or coastal paths.

Q: Are there any dog-friendly hiking trails in Errenteria?
A: While specific dog-friendly designations are not listed, many natural parks and trails in the Basque Country are generally welcoming to dogs, provided they are kept under control. It's always advisable to check local regulations for specific areas within Aiako Harria Natural Park or other protected zones.

Q: Can I combine hiking with exploring coastal areas near Errenteria?
A: Yes, Errenteria's proximity to the coast allows for excellent coastal hiking experiences. You can access routes like the Pasaia - San Sebastian coastal path, which offers stunning views and the chance to visit charming coastal towns and landmarks. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33905066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Pasaia Bay – Ancient Stone Aqueduct loop from Galtzaraborda&lt;/a&gt; is another great option for coastal landscapes.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced hikers in Errenteria?
A: Yes, Errenteria offers challenging routes for experienced hikers. The "Three Crowns" massif within Aiako Harria Natural Park, with peaks like Erroilbide, Txurrumurru, and Irumugarrieta, provides demanding ascents. Some passages on Jaizkibel Mountain, such as the "Grande faille," are also difficult and equipped with ropes, suitable for those with experience.
tips: Beautiful dolmen in a magical place 10 min. from Donosti. And hardly anyone knows him!

This path runs along the side of the mountain that has the abandoned Txoritokieta fortress. The path is very pretty with beautiful views, but if you have time to go up to the fortress it is very interesting.

Although now only a few ruins remain of what once was, a castle once stood here.
There is now an area with picnic tables, ideal for stopping for lunch.
If you get here by one of the trails that arrive, a little before you will also pass by a small pond.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on the San Marcos and Txoritokieta Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail offers a varied mountain biking experience. You can expect sections with quick and easy descents, some technical elements, and fast, winding paths through dark forests with roots, ruts, and large berms. Some parts may require good bike-handling skills due to technical features.

faqs.question: Are there any notable landmarks or viewpoints along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the loop passes by several interesting points. You can visit the ancient Dolmen de Aitzetako Txabala, enjoy the scenic View of the Txoritokieta Hillside, and explore the historic Ruins of Kutarro Castle.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to mountain bike this loop?
faqs.answer: While the trail can be enjoyed year-round, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant weather conditions for mountain biking in this region, with milder temperatures and less extreme heat or cold. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Does the trail intersect with other paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route incorporates sections of several official trails, including Errenteriatik Aiako Harrira (47% of the route), San Markos (46%), Txoritokieta (38%), Astigarraga. Ekialdeko Mugarriak (23%), and La Herradura (13%). It also briefly touches upon PR-Gi 1005 (5%).

faqs.question: Is the San Marcos and Txoritokieta Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: Generally, dogs are allowed on trails in the Lau-Haizeta Park area. However, it's always best to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas where wildlife might be present or near other trail users, to ensure safety and respect for the environment.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee for the Lau-Haizeta Park?
faqs.answer: Access to Lau-Haizeta Park for activities like mountain biking typically does not require a specific permit or entrance fee. However, always check local regulations before your visit, as rules can change.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the trail?
faqs.answer: Specific parking information for this exact loop isn't detailed, but trailheads in the Txoritokieta area often have designated parking spots. It's advisable to check local maps or community resources for the most convenient and official parking areas near the start of the loop.

faqs.question: Are there any cafes or pubs near the trail for refreshments?
faqs.answer: While there might not be establishments directly on the trail, the Txoritokieta area is close to Donostia/San Sebastian, Spain, where you'll find numerous cafes, restaurants, and pubs to refuel after your ride.

faqs.question: What should I bring for mountain biking this trail?
faqs.answer: Given the trail's 'difficult' rating and varied terrain, it's recommended to bring a well-maintained mountain bike, a helmet, appropriate protective gear, water, snacks, a basic repair kit, and a first-aid kit. A navigation device with the route loaded is also highly advisable.

faqs.question: Is this loop suitable for family-friendly mountain biking?
faqs.answer: Due to its 'difficult' rating and technical sections, this specific loop might be challenging for younger children or beginners. However, the broader Txoritokieta area offers a variety of trails, some of which are described as enjoyable with easier descents suitable for less experienced riders.

faqs.question: What kind of natural features can I expect to see?
faqs.answer: The trail winds through wooded areas and pine forests, offering a natural setting. You'll encounter typical forest flora and potentially some local wildlife. The View of the Txoritokieta Hillside provides expansive natural vistas.
